patch.value: The README should stay short for humans and package registries. Detailed operational docs belong in docs/ so agents can load only the relevant page for install, usage, release, or development work.
Restructure documentation for agent progressive disclosure

Reduce README to the essential install/run/documentation links and move usage, release, CI, development, SDK policy, temporary install testing, and secret review details into linked docs/ pages optimized for agents.

- README contains minimal package summary, install commands, and links.
- docs/README.md provides a short agent path.
- docs/usage.md contains CLI, extension, options, and TypeScript API details.
- docs/release.md contains release workflow, GitHub checks, npm checks, and versioning.
- docs/development.md contains layout, TypeScript policy, pm governance, temp install, and secret review guidance.
- package.json pm docs metadata and npm files include docs/.

value.author: codex
value.text: Documentation restructuring opened after README audit showed detailed usage and release material living in the top-level README.

value.author: codex
value.text: Keep all docs linked from README and docs/README.md.

value.author: codex
value.text: Short READMEs plus task-specific docs reduce context loading for agents.

value.author: codex
value.text: Verification: npm run pack:dry-run lists docs/development.md,docs/README.md,docs/release.md,and docs/usage.md in package contents.

patch.value: Moved detailed package documentation into docs/ and reduced README to minimal install/run/docs links.

README.md is now minimal; docs/README.md, docs/usage.md, docs/release.md, and docs/development.md contain the detailed progressive-disclosure documentation; package.json includes docs/ in npm files and pm docs metadata; npm pack --dry-run included all docs files.
The top-level README stays minimal while docs pages carry detailed usage, release, and development guidance linked for agent progressive disclosure.
